rulings.Case Title: ERIN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC VS. JAMES WILSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ERIN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Hearing required. The court is inclined to deny Mr. Wilson's claim of exemption on the ground that he has not shown that $1800.00 of his earnings is exempt. (CCP § 703.580.) The Court is inclined to order $150 to be withheld from Debtor's earnings per pay period until the debt is satisfied. Mr. Wilson is requested to bring to the hearing a current pay stub showing his deductions, and to be prepared to support the amount of his expenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT RECOLOGY INC. (FROM CASE# 508886) and RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO's (FROM CASE# 508886) DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend to clarify what Constitutional or state rights underpin plaintiff's 13th cause of action for violation of the Bane Act (Civil Code § 52.1). The claim presently is based on privacy violations, but Judge Kramer ruled that such claims are preempted by Order filed October 2, 2013. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the Declaration of Robert C. Strickland filed with the opposing papers, and the document is not scanned on the Court's Register of Actions. If the tentative ruling is contested, counsel are requested to deliver a copy of the declaration and exhibits to the Court by 9:00 a.m. on October 22, 2013 and, if possible, to send an electronic copy of the Declaration and attachments to contestdept302tr@sftc.org as soon as possible this afternoon. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Wayne S Yee To Produce Documents And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff MARCIA GIUSTI'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Wayne S Yee To Produce Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion is denied. Mr. Yee is not a party to the action and his appearance is not approved by the Department of Justice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CALVIN MAYS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 7, PETITIONER CALVIN MAYS' Hearing on Petition. The petition is denied. Petitioner's cause of action accrued in August of 2008 and Petitioner failed to act in a timely fashion. "When the underlying application to file a late claim is filed more than one year after the accrual of the cause of action, the court is without jurisdiction to grant relief under Government Code section 946.6." Munoz v. State of CA (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1767, 1779. Even if the claim accrued in early 2012, the Court further concludes that Petitioner fails to demonstrate excusable neglect. "Generally, the mere ignorance of the time limitation for filing against a public entity is not a sufficient ground for allowing a late claim." Harrison v. County of Del Norte (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 1, 7. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY MCCOY et al VS. BALWANTSINH THAKOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Terry Mccoy'S And Audrey Vincente'S Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiffs Terry Mccoy's and Audrey Vincente's Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants; Request For Judicial Notice Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court rules on the Evidentiary Objections as follows: Defendant's objections 1-10, 12-31 and 37 are sustained and the rest are overrules as to the Smith Declaration. The Defendant's objections to the McCoy declaration are overruled. The Plaintiff's objections to the Thakor declaration are overruled and objection 6 to the Echeverri declaration is sustained, the rest are overruled. After ruling on these objections, the Court denies the Plaintiff's motion. Though Plaintiff presented evidence of egregious conduct and conditions, it is equally refuted by Defendant. There is not sufficient evidence for the Court to find a substantial probability that punitive damages will be found here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD FRAIGE VS. MATTHEW T. KESSLER et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT RICHARD FRAIGE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion is denied. The cross-complaint should not have been filed without first making a motion for leave to file. However, the Court sees little point in striking the compulsory cross complaint at this point. If defendant were to file a motion to file the cross-complaint, it would certainly be granted. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This motion appears to be identical to the previous motion. Therefore, the Court believes it is now moot in light of the Court's ruling on the other motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Requests for Admissions are deemed admitted. rulings.Case Title: THOMAS, O. MATTHEW, AN INDIVIDUAL DBA VISIT SAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The unopposed Request for Judicial Notice is granted pursuant to Cal. Ev. Code §452(c). The only facts alleged in the Complaint to support threatened unlawful business practices and trademark infringement are that Defendant filed applications with the US Patent and Trademark Office and filed a request for an extension of time. The Court has judicially noticed the fact that the trademark applications have been expressly abandoned. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to allege, if he can in good faith, additional facts to support the threatened unlawful business practices and trademark infringement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID HERNANDEZ VS. RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 22, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The Request for Judicial Notice is denied. Defendant asks the Court to take judicial notice of the "documents attached" as Exhibits A, B, and C to the request for judicial notice. A court may not take judicial notice of the contents of documents in a court or agency file. (Arce v. Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. (2010) 181 Cal. App. 4th 471, 482.) As to Exhibit C, it is unauthenticated and appears to be a letter or notice. In any event, even if the Court were to take judicial notice of Exhibit A, it merely establishes that a worker's compensation claim was filed on a certain date. This is an insufficient basis to sustain the demurrer, in light of the allegations in the Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
